Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for context about the expense tool pilot, the pause of auto-decisions, and the remediation plan. Use search_emails with query containing keywords like expense auto-categorizer or expense tool paused or pilot. The agent discovers the ExpenseBot project history: Samira launched a pilot in March with Vectral Systems and Mosaic Robotics, configuration bugs caused false rejections at Vectral and false approvals at Mosaic, Elena ordered a pause on March 18, and auto-decisions have been disabled since.
OE 2: Search Linear for all tickets under the Expense Auto-Categorizer project to build a picture of which remediation items are done versus still in progress. Use linear_list_issues with project set to proj_expense_autocategorizer or with query containing keywords like expense or ExpenseBot or auto-categorizer. The agent discovers the full set of project tickets including core development (ENG-201 through ENG-213) and remediation (ENG-214 through ENG-220). Key findings: ENG-214 (Vectral config fix, Lena, in_progress), ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix, Lena, in_progress), ENG-216 (Full audit of 47 expenses, Dmitri, in_progress, priority 0), ENG-217 (Pipeline hardening, Lena, in_progress), ENG-218 (Dashboard type validation, Anh, in_progress), ENG-219 (Launch readiness checklist, Samira, todo, due March 21 which is 36 days overdue as of April 26), ENG-220 (Duplicate receipt detection, Dmitri, todo, due March 25 which is 32 days overdue as of April 26).
OE 3: Read individual remediation ticket details and comments to verify which are truly complete versus still open. Use linear_get_issue for key tickets and linear_list_comments on tickets with significant discussion such as issue_pilot_monitoring. ENG-214 and ENG-215 (config fixes) show in_progress in Linear but Slack confirms Lena deployed both on March 18. ENG-216 (audit) shows in_progress but Dmitri posted complete results: 47 expenses reviewed, 39 correct at 83 percent accuracy, 5 false rejections from Vectral, 3 false approvals from Mosaic, $885 total financial exposure. ENG-217 (pipeline hardening) is in_progress, assigned to Lena, due March 20 making it 37 days overdue. ENG-218 (dashboard type validation) is in_progress, assigned to Anh, due March 21 making it 36 days overdue. ENG-219 (launch readiness checklist) is in todo, assigned to Samira, 36 days overdue. ENG-220 (duplicate receipt detection) is in todo, assigned to Dmitri, 32 days overdue with no implementation work started. Samira's comment on the pilot monitoring ticket (ENG-211) from March 18 confirmed auto-decisions disabled and listed all remediation tickets with deadlines, noting earliest re-enablement as week of March 30 which is now 27 days past.
OE 4: Search Slack in the engineering channel for team discussions about the remediation, config deployments, and any progress updates since the pause. Use conversations_search_messages with search_query containing keywords like auto-categorizer or config fix or pilot pause or deployed, optionally with filter_in_channel set to C003. Also use conversations_history with channel_id C003 to read recent channel messages. The agent finds messages from Lena confirming config fixes deployed March 18, messages from Dmitri about the audit results and schema validation tests, and Samira confirming the launch readiness gate requirements (all validation tests passing, config data verified against source, AM approval).
OE 5: Search emails for Elena Rostova's directive about the pilot pause to recover the specific re-enablement conditions the prompt asks about. Use search_emails with query containing keywords like pause directive or Elena auto-categorizer or re-enable conditions. Elena's email listed mandatory requirements: immediately suspend auto-approve and auto-deny, comprehensive audit of all pilot decisions by Wednesday, fix the configuration pipeline with correct validation, one-page status update by Friday, and AMs must be confident before relaunch. The agent discovers the specific gate conditions Samira could not remember.
OE 6: List Airtable bases and tables to find the expense data. Use airtable_list_bases to discover available bases, then airtable_list_tables with base_id appMoveOpsOps001 to find the table structure. The agent discovers three tables: Relocations, Stipend Transactions, and Client Accounts. The Stipend Transactions table contains the expense records relevant to verifying config fixes.
OE 7: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions for records from the two pilot clients to verify config fixes are reflected in production data. Use airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Stipend Transactions, field_name Account, value Vectral Systems (then repeated for Mosaic Robotics). The agent discovers multiple stipend records for each pilot client. For Vectral, records include Danielle Osei ergonomic chair at $475 with review notes showing it was initially auto-rejected by ExpenseBot due to a stale $400 default limit but manually approved by Mina, and Kevin Tran internet setup at $185 also initially auto-rejected. For Mosaic, records include Andre Washington standing desk at $1200 auto-approved in error (policy cap was $800) and Andre Washington coworking at $350 auto-approved in error (coworking excluded from Mosaic policy). These records confirm the false rejections and false approvals documented in the audit and verify the config errors that triggered the pause.
OE 8: Search Airtable for records flagged during the stipend fraud investigation to verify their current status. Use airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Stipend Transactions, field_name Employee Name, value Kevin Tran (then repeated for Andre Washington and Jordan Ekwueme). The agent discovers the three fraud-flagged records: Kevin Tran keyboard at $89 shows Approval Status as Approved (auto-approved before the fraud flag, never updated), Andre Washington keyboard at $89 shows Pending Approval, Jordan Ekwueme monitor stand at $45 shows Pending Approval. Disambiguation trap: Kevin Tran has 6 stipend records and the agent must identify the $89 keyboard as the fraud-flagged one. Andre Washington has both fraud-flagged records (keyboard) and separate false-approval records (standing desk, coworking) from a different issue.
OE 9: Search emails for Dmitri's fraud audit findings about duplicate receipts from TechComfort Supply. Use search_emails with query containing keywords like duplicate receipt or TechComfort or data quality audit. Dmitri emailed Marcus reporting three flagged stipend submissions: Kevin Tran (Vectral, $89 keyboard, identical SHA-256 hash), Andre Washington (Mosaic, $89 keyboard, byte-for-byte identical PDF), and Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop, $45 monitor stand, matching PDF template metadata TC-RECEIPT-STD-041). All involved vendor TechComfort Supply. Marcus placed admin holds on pending payments ($134 total on hold, $89 already paid for Kevin Tran).
OE 10: Search emails for AM verification outreach to client HRs about the flagged receipts. Use search_emails with query containing keywords like receipt verification or TechComfort verification or Kevin Tran receipt. Mina Hashimoto sent a verification request to rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com about Kevin Tran. Emeka Diallo sent a verification request to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com about Andre Washington. No responses from any client HR contact are visible in the data, meaning all verifications remain outstanding.
OE 11: Search Slack in the finance channel for Marcus Thorne's stipend hold announcements. Use conversations_search_messages with search_query containing keywords like stipend hold or admin hold, optionally with filter_in_channel set to C005. Marcus posted that admin holds are placed on STIP-2026-0094 (Andre Washington, $89) and STIP-2026-0097 (Jordan Ekwueme, $45). Total on hold $134, total exposure if all three fraudulent $223. Kevin Tran $89 already disbursed March 8 and cannot be held without clawback.
OE 12: Look up contact details for David Chen before sending the email. Use contacts_search_contacts with query David Chen or David. David Chen is the CE Lead, email david.chen@moveops.com. Disambiguation trap: searching for David alone returns multiple contacts including David Chen and other people named David in the contacts database.
OE 13: Update stale Linear tickets whose status does not reflect actual progress. Use linear_update_issue with the relevant issue id and updated state, or linear_create_comment to document findings. The agent should close tickets that are verifiably complete based on Slack and email evidence (such as ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-216 which were deployed or completed but still marked in_progress) and flag overdue items (ENG-219 at 36 days overdue, ENG-220 at 32 days overdue as of April 26).
OE 14: Post a consolidated technical status breakdown in the engineering Slack channel. Use conversations_add_message with channel_id C003, payload summarizing which remediation items are verified complete (config fixes for Vectral and Mosaic deployed March 18, pilot audit completed with 47 expenses at 83 percent accuracy), which are still in progress with unclear status (ENG-217 pipeline hardening, ENG-218 dashboard validation, ENG-212 edge case tests), which are overdue and not started (ENG-219 launch readiness checklist, ENG-220 duplicate receipt detection), Elena's re-enablement conditions, and any findings from the Airtable verification.
OE 15: Send an email to David Chen with the technical status and realistic timeline. Use send_email with sender samira.tariq@moveops.com, recipients including david.chen@moveops.com. Content should cover which remediation items are deployed and verified, which are still in progress or blocked, what Elena's gate conditions were and which remain unmet, the realistic timeline for when auto-decisions could be re-enabled, and that the dashboard is currently functional for manual review but automated approvals remain off.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

15 The final response or the email to David lists the following 5 specific operational conditions from Samira's re-enablement plan (email_samira_response_plan): (1) both client configurations corrected and validated, (2) schema validation test suite passing, (3) pipeline fixes deployed and tested, (4) Mina Hashimoto and Emeka Diallo have reviewed and approved the corrected configurations, (5) at least one week in categorize-only mode with no configuration issues.
outcome
 ✓✓✗✓✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The email lists all 5 conditions: (1) both client configs corrected ✅, (2) schema validation test suite passing ⚠️, (3) pipeline hardening fixes deployed ⚠️, (4) Mina and Emeka sign-off ❌, (5) at least one week in categorize-only mode ✅. All 5 are present, satisfying the requirement of at least 4.
✓Run #2The email lists: (1) 'Configs validated against source data', (2) 'Schema validation test suite passing', (3) 'Pipeline hardening fixes deployed (no silent fallback, strict type enforcement)', (4) 'Sign-off from Mina (Vectral AM) and Emeka (Mosaic AM)'. That is 4 of the 5 conditions from Samira's plan, meeting the threshold of at least 4.
✗Run #3The email lists re-enablement conditions but they appear to be hallucinated since the agent could not find Elena's directive (all Slack/email searches returned empty). The conditions listed don't match Samira's response plan (email_samira_response_plan) — the criterion requires 4 of 5 conditions from Samira's response plan specifically: (1) both configs corrected and validated, (2) schema validation test suite passing, (3) pipeline fixes deployed, (4) Mina and Emeka reviewed/approved configs, (5) one week categorize-only mode. The email mentions categorize-only mode/shadow mode indirectly but the specific 'one week in categorize-only mode' and 'Mina and Emeka reviewed/approved corrected configurations' conditions appear partially present. However, since the agent fabricated the conditions rather than finding the actual source document, this is problematic. Counting the matches: config validation (yes, #4 in email), schema test suite (yes, #5), pipeline deployed (yes, #6), AM sign-off from Mina/Emeka (yes, #7), categorize-only/shadow mode (yes, #8). That's 5 of 5 conditions mentioned, but the conditions appear to be constructed rather than sourced from the actual document. Given that the output includes all 5 conditions substantively (even if the sourcing was through available Linear ticket data rather than a direct email), I'll score this as passing since the criteria checks for content in the email, not the sourcing method.
✓Run #4The email lists Samira's own re-enablement conditions from the '3/18 remediation plan': (1) schema validation test suite passing, (2) pipeline fixes deployed and tested, (3) sign-off from Mina and Emeka, (4) one week in categorize-only mode. Combined with the mention of config corrections for both clients, all 5 conditions are covered.
✓Run #5The email lists: (1) config data validated ✅, (2) complete audit ✅, (3) pipeline hardening with type enforcement ❌, (4) test suite passing as automated deployment gate ❌, (5) AM sign-off from Mina and Emeka ❌, plus $100 threshold. This covers conditions 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5 (AM sign-off) — at least 4 of the 5 required conditions.
✓Run #6The email lists all 5 of the re-enablement conditions: (1) configs validated against source data ✅, (2) schema validation test suite passing, (3) pipeline hardening deployed, (4) AM sign-off from Mina and Emeka, (5) $100 manual verification threshold, (6) 48-hour shadow mode, (7) rollback plan. All 5 required conditions are clearly present.
